title: List of all steps in order to cash cycle.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: List of all steps in order to cash cycle.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
1. Purchase of raw materials or inventory
2. Production and manufacturing
3. Sale of goods or services
4. Accounts receivable (invoicing customers)
5. Collection of payments from customers
6. Accounts payable (paying suppliers)
7. Inventory turnover (selling inventory)
8. Cash receipts from sales
9. Payment of expenses (rent, utilities, salaries, etc.)
10. Cash balance (available cash on hand)

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: List of all activities involved in cash application process

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
1. Receiving customer payments (e.g. checks, wire transfers, ACH transfers, credit card transactions)
2. Recording customer payments in the accounting system
3. Matching customer payments to open invoices or accounts receivable balances
4. Resolving payment discrepancies (e.g. underpayments, overpayments, unpaid or short-paid invoices)
5. Applying cash to customer accounts or invoices using automated or manual processes
6. Reconciling cash receipts with bank statements and accounting records
7. Performing data analysis to identify trends or issues (e.g. identifying late payments, average payment times, or payment anomalies)
8. Investigating and resolving customer inquiries or disputes related to payment
9. Posting adjustments to customer accounts or invoices as necessary
10. Preparing and submitting reports about cash application activities and results.
